name: SAPA
mission: Sudanese American Physicians Association (SAPA) is a not-for-profit, scientific professional, non-partisan, and humanitarian membership-based organization established in January 2019
theoryOfChange: SAPA (Sudanese American Physicians Association) utilizes a documented approach to planning and measuring the impact of its projects, which aligns with a results framework. Their 'SAPA Project Proposal' document, available on their website, includes sections for 'Project Goals,' 'Project Objectives,' 'Expected results,' 'Deliverables,' 'Benefits,' 'Problems to be resolved,' 'Project Activities,' and 'Key Performance Indicators (KPI).' This framework details how their activities are intended to lead to specific outcomes and how these outcomes will be monitored and evaluated. Additionally, SAPA's overall strategy is described as a 'comprehensive, holistic approach to healthcare delivery' and their 'programming framework includes pillars such as Direct Health Service Delivery, Health System Resiliency, WASH, Food and Nutrition, and Protection.' A report detailing their emergency response and preparedness programming also serves as a 'framework for SAPA's programming moving forward.'
scoreSummary: Sudanese American Physicians Association shows exceptional impact and strong alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://sapa-usa.org/zakat (Source: https://sapa-usa.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 93% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

all_citations.claim: SAPA holds a 97.0/100 rating with a program expense ratio of 81.2% and fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised.
The organization provides multiple donation options and giving methods for its healthcare and emergency programs.
SAPA is Zakat-eligible and follows specific policies to distribute funds according to asnaf categories.
The organization reported total revenue of $7,254,154 and holds 4.3 months of working capital.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAPA demonstrates exceptional financial health and high impact directness, particularly for donors seeking urgent global health interventions. Its Zakat-eligible status and low overhead costs make it a highly efficient vehicle for humanitarian aid.
areas_for_improvement: Expanding long-term healthcare institutional capacity beyond immediate crisis response
Increasing public transparency regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics across diverse programs

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment for Muslim donors due to its Zakat-eligible status and focus on high-urgency global health crises [3]. Its track record of accountability is reflected in a Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100 and a fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: SAPA achieves a high impact rating through direct service delivery, maintaining a program expense ratio of 81.2% and a total revenue of $7,254,154 dedicated to healthcare and hunger relief [1][4]. The organization demonstrates high evidence quality in its emergency response and healthcare strategy in Sudan and Chad [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Sudanese American Physicians Association (SAPA) strengthens healthcare infrastructure and provides life-saving emergency services to communities in crisis across Sudan and Chad.
strengths: High financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 81.2%
Excellent fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.05 to raise every $1
Strong short-term liquidity with 4.3 months of working capital
Direct impact model with a high Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100
baseline_narrative.summary: SAPA is a professional and humanitarian organization dedicated to building resilient healthcare systems through emergency medical services, hunger relief, and strategic infrastructure support [1][4]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through programs that serve vulnerable populations in accordance with established asnaf categories [3].

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="4">81.2% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="1">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>.
SAPA holds a <cite id="1">97.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite> and a Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid, supported by a <cite id="4">12-member independent board</cite>.
The charity achieves an <cite id="1">impact score of 98/100</cite> from Charity Navigator, reflecting its effectiveness in direct service delivery.

strengths_deep_dive: SAPA's financial resilience is a core strength, evidenced by its <cite id="1">4.3 months of working capital</cite>, which provides a significant buffer for operations in volatile conflict zones. This liquidity is paired with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 263.1%</cite>, demonstrating the organization's ability to mobilize resources rapidly during the escalating crisis in Sudan. Furthermore, the organization tracks <cite id="4">13 distinct outcome metrics</cite> through Candid, ensuring that its healthcare and hunger relief programs are data-driven and transparent.
rich_narrative.summary: The Sudanese American Physicians Association (SAPA) is a professional and humanitarian organization that has rapidly scaled its operations to address the critical healthcare vacuum in Sudan and neighboring regions. Founded in <cite id="4">2019</cite>, the organization has transitioned from a professional network into a primary provider of <cite id="4">emergency medical services, hunger relief, and WASH programs</cite>. SAPA's operational model is distinguished by its high efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="1">97/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. This rating is underpinned by a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 81.2%</cite> in its most recent fiscal reporting, ensuring that donor contributions are directed almost entirely toward frontline medical interventions. The organization reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $3,502,228</cite> in 2023, representing a remarkable <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 263.1%</cite>. Beyond immediate crisis response, SAPA works to <cite id="4">build the core functions of a health care system</cite> that can withstand ongoing instability. As a <cite id="3">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, SAPA provides a verified channel for Muslim donors to support <cite id="3">vulnerable populations (asnaf)</cite> through specialized medical and nutritional programs in Muslim-majority regions like Sudan and Chad.
